Currently, vLLM assumes all Eagle3 heads use aux hidden states. This PR removes this assumption and checks from the draft model config.

vllm.title: [BugFix] Fix duplicate req id tool-call race condition
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The front-end built-in tool calling code can make successive generate calls with the same top-level request id.
This can cause a problem with async scheduling since the prior request can still be present in the engine (in the case of stop/eos tokens for example, we generate one additional token).
Th

vllm.title: [fix][cpu] Use a SwigluOAI impl which supports interleaved gate-up wei
vllm.author: fadara01
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
[fix][cpu] Use a SwigluOAI impl which supports interleaved gate-up wei

Purpose
Current impl of swigluoai_and_mul for CPU assumes that gate-up weights have been de-interleaved at load time, which is not the case and as a result gpt-oss generates garbage.
The new impl we dispatch to in this PR is the

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Pin torchgeo dependency for AMD
vllm.author: rjrock
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Without pinning torchgeo, terratorch defaults to installing torchgeo==0.2.1. At that version, though, calling python -c "import terratorch" results in the below error.

ImportError: cannot import name 'NonGeoDataset' from 'torchgeo.datasets'

Pinning torchgeo to 0.7.0 resolves that error, an

vllm.title: Allow oot custom compiler extension via CompilerInterface
vllm.author: wxsIcey
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
From vLLM-Ascend, I am trying to enable some graph fusion operations by extending the vLLM core. However, it seems that the compiled backend is currently coupled with the "inductor" backend, and not all hardware backends support this backend. In vLLM-Ascend, pattern matcher for the inductor

vllm.title: [Perf] use cpu all reduce to avoid sync when async_scheduling & dp > 1
vllm.author: izhuhaoran
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Disable NCCL-based DP synchronization whenever async scheduling is enabled. This forces _synchronize_dp_ranks to use the CPU all-reduce path , which avoids GPU tensor copy staging and the ensuing NCCL all-reduce sync that was stalling the async scheduling pipeline.

  
    
      vllm/vllm/v

vllm.title: [Spec-Decode][DP] EAGLE Support DP>1
vllm.author: Flechman
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
When using DP>1, the set_forward_context gathers the number of tokens across all DP ranks to get static batch size for all ranks for CudaGRAPH+EP compatibility.
When using EAGLE and DP>1 with num_speculative_tokens=k, and sending a single request, one DP rank will handle that request (going
